candidates.name: Ned Lamont
candidates.notes: Two-term governor (since 2019). Wealthy businessman and cable TV entrepreneur (grandson of J.P. Morgan partner). Signed CT assault weapons ban expansion post-Sandy Hook era. Pro-choice. Supports sanctuary policies. Opposes school vouchers. Signed executive order protecting abortion access and gender-affirming care.

profile.religion: Episcopalian (attends Round Hill Community Church, Greenwich)
profile.education: Harvard College (BA, Sociology); Yale School of Management (MBA)
profile.background: Businessman and cable television entrepreneur; ran for U.S. Senate in 2006; became 89th governor of Connecticut in 2019.

candidates.name: Richard Blumenthal
candidates.notes: Senator since 2011. Former CT Attorney General (20 years). Heritage Action 0% current, 5% lifetime. NRA F rated. Son of German Jewish refugee who fled Nazis. Strong gun control advocate. Pro-choice. Supports DREAM Act. Supports Equality Act. Known for aggressive consumer protection lawsuits. Controversy over military service claims.

profile.background: Former Connecticut Attorney General for 20 years. U.S. Marine Corps Reserve veteran. Former federal prosecutor and aide to Senator Abraham Ribicoff. Clerked for Supreme Court Justice Harry Blackmun.
profile.office: 503 Hart Senate Office Building

aipac-track.title: TrackAIPAC — Connecticut delegation
aipac-track.excerpt: Aggregates every AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby contribution each member of Congress has received, sourced directly from FEC filings and OpenSecrets. Matches the RESOLUTE Citizen America-First question 3: foreign-backed lobby donations.

candidates.name: Chris Murphy
candidates.notes: Senator since 2013. Heritage Action 0% current, 6% lifetime. NRA F rated. Leading Senate voice for gun control after Sandy Hook shooting (which was in his district as House member). Conducted 15-hour filibuster for gun control. Pro-choice. Supports DREAM Act. Supports Equality Act. Raised Congregationalist.

profile.education: Williams College (BA History), University of Connecticut School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Former Connecticut state legislator and U.S. House member. Leading gun control advocate after Sandy Hook shooting in his district. Real estate and healthcare attorney. In Senate since 2013.
profile.office: 136 Hart Senate Office Building

Strong gun control advocate. Pro-choice. Supports DREAM Act. Supports Equality Act. Known for aggressive consumer protection lawsuits. Controversy over military service claims.
